## Schema.plugin() method

Adds a plugin to the schema, plugin will not be applied until the applyPlugins method is called.

**Signature:**

```typescript
plugin(fn: PluginFn<RawDocType, TInstanceMethods, TVirtualProperties, TStaticMethods, HydratedDoc>, opt?: any): this;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter


</th><th>

Type


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

fn


</td><td>

[PluginFn](./iodm.pluginfn.md)<!-- -->&lt;RawDocType, TInstanceMethods, TVirtualProperties, TStaticMethods, HydratedDoc&gt;


</td><td>

plugin function to apply to the schema


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

opt


</td><td>

any


</td><td>

_(Optional)_ options to pass to the plugin function


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

this

schema instance for chaining

## Remarks

applyPlugins method will be called internally by the top level model when the model is initialized, so there is no need to call it manually in most cases.

## Example


```ts
const pluginFn = (schema, options) => {
 // plugin implementation
}

const userSchema = new Schema({
 firstName: String,
 lastName: String,
});

userSchema.plugin(pluginFn, { option1: 'value1' });
```
